title: Design System: Human-Machine Intelligent Systems Lab

display.purpose: Hero titles only. Italic + Coral emphasis on a single phrase per hero.

title.purpose: Section h2 and Spread headings in Fraunces serif.

body.purpose: Primary reading text in Plus Jakarta Sans, capped at 65-75ch.

subhead.purpose: h4 and inline emphasis in Plus Jakarta Sans medium.

label.purpose: Eyebrows, h3 section labels, navigation, tags. The site's primary 'small loud' element.

mono.purpose: Code, file paths, technical identifiers in JetBrains Mono.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Interactive image surfaces on hover. Studio Slate at low opacity.

shadows.name: coral-focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard focus indicator for interactive elements. Replaces browser default outline.

shadows.name: coral-glow
shadows.purpose: Hover halo for portraits and focal photographs.

motion.name: fast
motion.purpose: Default state-change easing (ease-out-quart). Hover, focus, color shifts.

motion.name: hero-grid-drift
motion.purpose: Slow drift of the hero coral dot-grid. Disable under prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Coral-filled CTA. Used for primary actions, typically inside the hero or at the end of a Spread.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #F97171; color: #F5F0E8; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.88rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.5px; padding: 11px 26px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), transform 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), box-shadow 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #F84B4B; box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(249, 113, 113, 0.4); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(249, 113, 113, 0.30); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Ghost button on dark surfaces. Used inside the hero alongside Primary.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: transparent; color: #F5F0E8; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.88rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.5px; padding: 11px 26px; border: 1.5px solid rgba(245, 240, 232, 0.3); border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: #FCA5A5; color: #FCA5A5; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(249, 113, 113, 0.30); }

components.name: Big Link
components.description: Plain coral text link with a hidden bottom border that appears on hover. For 'read more' affordances and inline secondary navigation.
components.css: .ds-big-link { display: inline-block; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.9rem; text-decoration: none; color: #F97171; border-bottom: 1.5px solid transparent; padding-bottom: 2px; transition: color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), border-color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-big-link:hover { color: #F84B4B; border-bottom-color: #F97171; } .ds-big-link:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(249, 113, 113, 0.30); border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Tag (Coral Pill)
components.description: Coral-tinted pill for topics, methods, or status keywords. Replaces the deprecated lavender tag.
components.css: .ds-tag-coral { display: inline-block; padding: 4px 13px; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1.5px solid rgba(249, 113, 113, 0.4); color: #F84B4B; background: rgba(249, 113, 113, 0.12); font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.3px; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), border-color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-tag-coral:hover { background: rgba(249, 113, 113, 0.22); border-color: #F97171; }

components.name: Eyebrow Label
components.description: Small uppercase label above a title. Used in the hero and at the top of every Spread.
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { display: inline-block; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 2.5px; text-transform: uppercase; color: #F97171; padding: 4px 16px; border: 1px solid rgba(249, 113, 113, 0.4); border-radius: 9999px; background: rgba(249, 113, 113, 0.08); }

components.name: Navigation Link
components.description: Header navigation link. Stone at rest, Coral with bottom border on hover and active.
components.css: .ds-nav-link { display: inline-block; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 1.2px; color: #78716C; text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; transition: color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), border-color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-nav-link:hover, .ds-nav-link[aria-current="page"] { color: #F97171; border-bottom-color: #F97171; } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(249, 113, 113, 0.30); border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Portrait
components.description: Circular framed photo of a team member with optional role badge. Frame turns Coral on hover with a faint glow ring.
components.html: Dr. Sample Name
components.css: .ds-portrait { display: inline-flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 150px; margin: 20px; text-decoration: none; } .ds-portrait__image { display: inline-block; width: 150px; height: 150px; border-radius: 9999px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 4px 8px rgba(41, 37, 36, 0.08), 0 1px 2px rgba(41, 37, 36, 0.04); border: 2px solid transparent; transition: border-color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), box-shadow 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-portrait__image img { object-fit: cover; width: 100%; height: 100%; transition: transform 0.4s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-portrait:hover .ds-portrait__image { border-color: #F97171; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(249, 113, 113, 0.15), 0 4px 8px rgba(41, 37, 36, 0.08); } .ds-portrait:hover .ds-portrait__image img { transform: scale(1.05); } .ds-portrait__name { margin-top: 10px; text-align: center; line-height: 1.2; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; color: #292524; }

components.name: Spread (Image + Text Article Row)
components.description: The signature content pattern. Large image on one side, text article on the other, with a slight overlap at the seam. Alternates left/right per instance for rhythm. Replaces card grids for projects, research, and team.
components.html:
PROJECT 03 / 2025

Wearable rhythm monitoring for at-home cardiac care

A collaboration with the cardiology unit at the regional hospital, asking whether a low-cost wrist sensor can flag the onset of atrial fibrillation between clinic visits. Patients keep the device for thirty days; we keep the model honest.

Read the paper
components.css: .ds-spread { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 0; align-items: center; max-width: 1100px; margin: 80px auto; padding: 0 24px; } .ds-spread__image { position: relative; z-index: 1; aspect-ratio: 4 / 3; overflow: hidden; transition: transform 0.4s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-spread__image img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; display: block; } .ds-spread__text { position: relative; z-index: 2; padding: 32px 32px 32px 0; max-width: 65ch; } .ds-spread--image-left .ds-spread__image { grid-column: 1; grid-row: 1; } .ds-spread--image-left .ds-spread__text { grid-column: 2; grid-row: 1; margin-left: -80px; padding: 40px 0 40px 32px; background: #FFF7ED; } .ds-spread--image-right .ds-spread__image { grid-column: 2; grid-row: 1; } .ds-spread--image-right .ds-spread__text { grid-column: 1; grid-row: 1; margin-right: -80px; padding: 40px 32px 40px 0; background: #FFF7ED; text-align: right; } .ds-spread__title { font-family: 'Fraunces', Georgia, serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.6rem; line-height: 1.3; letter-spacing: -0.3px; color: #292524; margin: 16px 0 16px 0; } .ds-spread__body { font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.75; letter-spacing: 0.01em; color: #292524; margin: 0 0 20px 0; max-width: 65ch; } @media (max-width: 900px) { .ds-spread { grid-template-columns: 1fr; gap: 16px; } .ds-spread__image, .ds-spread__text { grid-column: 1 !important; grid-row: auto !important; margin: 0 !important; padding: 24px 0 !important; text-align: left !important; background: transparent !important; } }

narrative.overview:
The HMIS Lab website is an open studio. A research practice with the door propped open: visitors walk through and see what is on the wall, what is on the bench, who is working there. The visual system serves that openness. Warm enough that an undergraduate feels welcomed. Structured enough that the work shows clearly. Restrained enough that the chrome never competes with what is being shown.

The system rejects two opposing failure modes. On one side, the AI-startup aesthetic: neon on black, glassmorphism, gradient text, generic neural-net iconography. On the other side, default-Bootstrap-academic: three-column footer, stock-photo hero, card grids of equivalent items.

The going-forward content pattern is the spread: a large image on one side, text on the other, with a slight overlap at the seam. Spreads alternate sides down a long page. Storytelling, not cataloging.
keyCharacteristics: A single warm coral as the only true accent, scarce on purpose.
Stone-and-cream neutrals tinted toward the coral hue. No pure white or black.
Editorial-flat surfaces. Shadows only as a response to user state.
Fraunces (variable serif) for display and headlines, Plus Jakarta Sans for body and UI, JetBrains Mono for code.
Spreads with seam overlap as the primary content pattern; cards are deprecated.
Restraint is the discipline; warmth is the personality.

rules.name: The One Coral Rule
rules.body: Coral appears on no more than 10% of any given screen. Its rarity is the point. It is the lab's only loud color, and it earns its visibility by being scarce.

rules.name: The No Lavender Rule
rules.body: Purple and lavender are forbidden. The category-reflex risk for an ML lab site is 'AI startup with purple gradients.' This site refuses that family entirely. The previous lavender accent (#C4B5FD) and its purple text counterpart (#6B46E0) are deprecated; migrate any usage to a Coral-tinted equivalent.

rules.name: The Tinted Neutrals Rule
rules.body: Every neutral in the system is tinted toward warm-stone (hue ~60°) or coral (hue ~22°). Pure neutrals (#000, #FFF, #888) are forbidden — they read as cold against the warm palette and break the system's identity.

rules.name: The Italic Coral Rule
rules.body: When a phrase in a Display title needs emphasis, set it in italic Fraunces and color it Coral Light (on dark) or Coral (on light). One phrase per page maximum.

rules.name: The Body 65ch Rule
rules.body: Body text wraps at 65-75ch. Wider lines fail readers; narrower lines fragment thought.

rules.name: The Uppercase-Label Rule
rules.body: Uppercase is reserved for the Label role. Headlines, titles, and body never go uppercase. The 2px letter-spacing on Labels is the visual signature; do not reduce it.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows appear only as a response to user state — hover, focus, drag.

rules.name: The Warm-Brown Shadow Rule
rules.body: All structural shadows use rgba(41, 37, 36, 0.04-0.10), Studio Slate at low opacity. Coral glows use rgba(249, 113, 113, 0.15-0.30). Never rgb(0, 0, 0); never blue-gray.

dos: Do lead with the Spread pattern: image one side, text the other, slight seam overlap.
Do alternate spread sides for rhythm down long pages.
Do use Coral on no more than 10% of any screen.
Do tint every neutral toward warm-stone (60°) or coral (22°).
Do use Fraunces italic + Coral for one moment of emphasis per hero.
Do use Notebook Sage only for 'in-progress' or 'live' status, never as decoration.
Do keep surfaces flat at rest. Reserve shadow for hover and focus state only.
Do treat alt text as content for every research and team photo.
Do distinguish sections through alternating Warm Linen / Pale Honey background and vertical rhythm, not through cards.
donts: Don't use lavender, purple, violet, magenta, or any cool accent. Migrate the current $secondary: #C4B5FD and #6B46E0 tag-text usages to the Coral tag spec.
Don't use card grids as the primary content pattern for projects, research, or team. Use Spreads.
Don't use top-stripe or side-stripe colored borders on cards or list items.
Don't use gradient text or gradient underlines.
Don't use neon-on-black, glassmorphism, or generic neural-net hero illustrations.
Don't introduce 'Get started' SaaS-landing CTAs, feature-checkmark grids, social-proof bars, or hero-metric templates.
Don't use em dashes in copy.
Don't add cards as the lazy answer for new content.
Don't animate CSS layout properties. Use transform and opacity. Ease out with exponential curves.
